stories.title: Dodgers Hit 5 HRs in 14-2 Monday Blowout — Rushing, Ohtani, Hernandez, Freeman Unload (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
The Dodgers hit five home runs on Monday.

Dalton Rushing: 2. Teoscar Hernandez: 1. Freddie Freeman: 1. Shohei Ohtani: 1.

Final score: Dodgers 14, Blue Jays 2. The defending champs are not messing around. #Dodgers

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Dalton Rushing went 4-for-4 with two home runs Monday — his first career multi-HR game.

That performance came in the middle of a 14-2 Dodgers blowout over Toronto that featured five total HRs and 14 runs.

The Dodgers offense is fully awake. #Dodgers #MLB

article.headline: Dodgers Crush Blue Jays 14-2 With Five HRs — Dalton Rushing's First Career Multi-Homer Game Headlines Blowout

stories.title: Betts Update — "Light Years Ahead," Dodgers Optimistic on Return Timeline (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Mookie Betts: "light years ahead" of where he expected to be.

Dodgers say his oblique strain recovery could be faster than the typical 4-6 weeks.

Still on the 10-day IL — but the news is better than feared. #Dodgers

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Two stars, two IL stints, both with better news than expected:

Mookie Betts (Dodgers) — oblique, recovery ahead of schedule.
Juan Soto (Mets) — grade 1 calf strain, back in 2-3 weeks.

The injury picture is improving. #MLB

article.headline: Betts and Soto Updates: "Light Years Ahead" and Grade 1 — Both Stars Have Better News Than Expected

stories.title: Aaron Judge Continues Career HR Chase — 3 HRs in 7 Games, Yankees Lead AL East (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Aaron Judge hit his third home run of 2026 in the Yankees home opener.

387 feet. 101.2 mph exit velocity. Off Eury Pérez. New York won 8-2.

He entered 2026 with 368 career HRs. He needs 32 more to reach 400. #Yankees #MLB

article.headline: Aaron Judge Homers in the Yankees Home Opener — The Career HR Chase Is On in the Bronx

stories.title: Konnor Griffin — Rough Start, Long View (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Konnor Griffin is 1-for-9 in his first four MLB games.

He was dropped in the batting order. He's 19 years old and adjusting to the big leagues in real time.

The Pirates remain committed. Patience is part of the plan with the No. 1 prospect in baseball. #LetsGoBucs

stories.title: Shohei Ohtani — 36-Game On-Base Streak, Power Numbers, Two-Way Dominance (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Shohei Ohtani on April 6: solo home run, Dodgers 14-2.

Shohei Ohtani on March 31: 6 scoreless innings, 1 hit, 6 Ks on 87 pitches.

He's also in the middle of a 36-game on-base streak.

The best player in baseball is doing what the best player in baseball does. #Dodgers

article.headline: Shohei Ohtani Is Doing Everything Again — 36-Game On-Base Streak, Dominant Pitching Debut, Monday HR

stories.title: DeLauter and Murakami — Two Historic Rookie Starts in the Same Week (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Chase DeLauter: 5 HRs in 8 games.
Munetaka Murakami: 4 HRs in 8 games (most by a Japanese-born player in their first 8 MLB games).

Both in the first two weeks of the season.

The 2026 rookie class is already one for the books. #MLB #Guardians #WhiteSox

stories.title: Early MLB Standings — Dodgers, Yankees Out Front; Red Sox Struggling (Tier 3)

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
Ten days into the 2026 MLB season:

Dodgers and Yankees are both around 7-2 and look like the class of baseball.
Brewers are a pleasant surprise.
Red Sox are around 2-8 and already in trouble.

The early picture is taking shape. #MLB
